title: Amritaghateswarar-Abirami Temple,Thirukkadaiyur
tagline: Amritaghateswarar-Abirami Temple,Thirukkadaiyur — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Amrithakadeeswarar Abhirami Temple (also called Abhirami temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva in his manifestation as Kalantaka and his wife Parvati as Abhirami.
overview.description: Amrithakadeeswarar Abhirami Temple (also called Abhirami temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva in his manifestation as Kalantaka and his wife Parvati as Abhirami. There is a Shrine for Maha Vishnu as Amrithanarayana and his consort Mahalakshmi as Amrithavalli. It is located in Thirukkadaiyur (Thirukkadavur), 21 km East of Mayiladuthurai, Tamil Nadu in India. This temple is associated with the legend of Shiva saving his young devotee, Markendeya from death, and the tale of a saint, Abirami Pattar a devotee of the presiding goddess.

topPlaces.name: Thirukkadaiyur
topPlaces.description: Thirukkadaiyur (Thirukadavur) is a village on the east coast of Tamil Nadu, about 300 km south of Chennai and 15 km north of Karaikal. The history of the village is associated with the legends of Markandeya and Abirami Pattar.

topPlaces.name: Manjalar River
topPlaces.description: Manjalar originates from Palani Hills, Tamil Nadu and runs towards east and joins the Vaigai River near Koottathu. There are nine anicuts and nine tanks in this sub-basin.

topPlaces.name: Akkur, Mayiladuthurai
topPlaces.description: Akkur is a small town in Mayiladuthurai district, Tamil Nadu, India 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from Mayiladuthurai town. Akkur is located 260 km from the state capital, Chennai.

topPlaces.name: Thanthondreeswarar Temple, Akkur
topPlaces.description: Akkur Thanthondreeswarar Temple(ஆக்கூர் தான்தோன்றீசுவரர் கோயில் ]) is a Hindu temple located at Akkur in Mayiladuthurai district of Tamil Nadu, India. The presiding deity is Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Willem Leyel's siege of Dansborg
topPlaces.description: The siege of Dansborg (Danish; belejringen af Dansborg) alternatively the siege of Fort Dansborg (Danish; belejringen af Fort Dansborg) sometimes also referred to as Willem Leyel's siege of Dansborg (Danish; William Leyels belejring af Dansborg), was a siege initiated by traveler and seafarer, Willem (Danish; William) Leyel, against the men loyal to governor Bernt Pessart. The siege was concluded after the men at Dansborg opened the gates for Willem Leyel, surrendered, and accepted Leyel as the new governor.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Dansborg (1644)
topPlaces.description: The siege of Dansborg (Danish; belejringen af Dansborg) or the siege of Fort Dansborg (Danish; belejringen af Fort Dansborg), was a short siege lasting from 20 to 30 December 1644, between general Tiagepule of Thanjavur and the Danish command at Fort Dansborg. The conflict started over the Danish rejection of the general's demand to tax Tranquebar, and as a result, a series of confrontations followed.

topPlaces.name: Thalachangadu
topPlaces.description: Thalachangadu is a historical village located in Mayiladuthurai district, Tamil Nadu, India, on the bank of the Cauvery River. Its southern boundary is the Rajendran Channel, on the north side is the Cauvery River, on the east side is MelaPerumPallam and on the western side is Natraja Pillai Chavadi.

topPlaces.name: Tillali Massacre
topPlaces.description: The Tillali Massacre (Danish: Massakren i Tillali), or the Battle of Tillali (Danish: Slaget ved Tillali) was a confrontation on 30 June 1756 during the Cattle War, between the Danish command at the village of Tillali (Thillaiyadi) and the Raja of Thanjavur, Pratap Singh. The confrontation resulted in a Thanjavurian victory and a subsequent massacre of most Danish troops.

items.note: Thirukkadaiyur (Thirukadavur) is a village on the east coast of Tamil Nadu, about 300 km south of Chennai and 15 km north of Karaikal. The history of the village is associated with the legends of Markandeya and Abirami Pattar.
Manjalar originates from Palani Hills, Tamil Nadu and runs towards east and joins the Vaigai River near Koottathu. There are nine anicuts and nine tanks in this sub-basin.
Akkur is a small town in Mayiladuthurai district, Tamil Nadu, India 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from Mayiladuthurai town. Akkur is located 260 km from the state capital, Chennai.
Akkur Thanthondreeswarar Temple(ஆக்கூர் தான்தோன்றீசுவரர் கோயில் ]) is a Hindu temple located at Akkur in Mayiladuthurai district of Tamil Nadu, India. The presiding deity is Shiva.

The siege of Dansborg (Danish; belejringen af Dansborg) alternatively the siege of Fort Dansborg (Danish; belejringen af Fort Dansborg) sometimes also referred to as Willem Leyel's siege of Dansborg (Danish; William Leyels belejring af Dansborg), was a siege initiated by traveler and seafarer, Willem (Danish; William) Leyel, against the men loyal to governor Bernt Pessart. The siege was concluded after the men at Dansborg opened the gates for Willem Leyel, surrendered, and accepted Leyel as the new governor.

The siege of Dansborg (Danish; belejringen af Dansborg) or the siege of Fort Dansborg (Danish; belejringen af Fort Dansborg), was a short siege lasting from 20 to 30 December 1644, between general Tiagepule of Thanjavur and the Danish command at Fort Dansborg. The conflict started over the Danish rejection of the general's demand to tax Tranquebar, and as a result, a series of confrontations followed.

Thalachangadu is a historical village located in Mayiladuthurai district, Tamil Nadu, India, on the bank of the Cauvery River. Its southern boundary is the Rajendran Channel, on the north side is the Cauvery River, on the east side is MelaPerumPallam and on the western side is Natraja Pillai Chavadi.

The Tillali Massacre (Danish: Massakren i Tillali), or the Battle of Tillali (Danish: Slaget ved Tillali) was a confrontation on 30 June 1756 during the Cattle War, between the Danish command at the village of Tillali (Thillaiyadi) and the Raja of Thanjavur, Pratap Singh. The confrontation resulted in a Thanjavurian victory and a subsequent massacre of most Danish troops.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Amritaghateswarar-Abirami Temple,Thirukkadaiyur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Amritaghateswarar-Abirami Temple,Thirukkadaiyur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Amritaghateswarar-Abirami Temple,Thirukkadaiyur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (126 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (127 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Amritaghateswarar-Abirami Temple,Thirukkadaiyur?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Amritaghateswarar-Abirami Temple,Thirukkadaiyur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Amritaghateswarar-Abirami Temple,Thirukkadaiyur?
faq.a: Stays in Amritaghateswarar-Abirami Temple,Thirukkadaiyur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Amritaghateswarar-Abirami Temple,Thirukkadaiyur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thirukkadaiyur, Manjalar River, Akkur, Mayiladuthurai, Thanthondreeswarar Temple, Akkur, Willem Leyel's siege of Dansborg.
